revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hayasuihime-jinja]][[ja:早吸日女神社]] {{Expand Japanese|早吸日女神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hayasuihime Shrine | native_name = 早吸日女神社 | image = Hayasuihime-jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hayasuihime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.250091|131.878921}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Magatsuhinokami|lt=Magatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|zh|禍津日神|qid=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hayasuihime Shrine'''|早吸日女神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bungo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần Province|ja|豊後国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11633695|1=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 5 ** P155: {{ill|Q11566947|ltq=Q11566947|qid=Q11566947|1=Honoo-Honome Shrine}} ** P156: (مدينة)|azb|اوئیتا|az|Oita|ban|Ōita, Ōita|be|Оіта (горад)|bg|Оита|ca|Ōita (ciutat)|ceb|Ōita (kapital sa prepektura sa Hapon)|ce|Оита (гӀала)|cs|Óita|cy|Ōita|de|Ōita|el|Όιτα|en|Ōita (city)|envoyage|Oita|eo|Oita (urbo)|es|Ōita|et|Ōita|eu|Ōita|fa|اوئیتا|favoyage|اوئیتا|fi|Ōita|fivoyage|Ōita|fr|Ōita|frvoyage|Oita|ga|Ōita|gl|Ōita|he|אויטה (יפן)|hy|Օիտա|id|Ōita, (город)|runews|Категория:Оита (город)|sco|Ōita, Ōita|sc|Ōita|sh|Ōita|simple|Ōita (city)|sr|Оита|sv|Ōita|sw|Ōita, Ōita|szl|Ōita|tg|Оита|th|โออิตะ (เมือง)|tl|Lungsod ng Ōita|tr|Ōita|tt|Оита (шәһәр)|uk|Оїта|ur|اوئیتا|vi|Ōita (thành phố)|war|Ōita (syudad)|wuu|大分市|zh_classical|大分市|zh_min_nan|Ôita-chhī|zh_yue|大分市|zh|大分市|qid=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}} * {{ill|Q864148|ltq=Q864148|qid=Q864148|1=Bungo Province}} * {{ill|Amabe district|lt=Amabe district|ja|海部郡 (豊後国)|zh|海部郡 (豐後國)|qid=Q11151384|1=Amabe district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183315 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4320005002063 == Commons category (P373) == * Hayasuihime-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大分県大分市佐賀関3336-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hayasuihime-jinja}} [[JA:早吸日女神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11511196}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Saganoseki, Oita City, Oita Prefecture|shrine in Kamae, Saiki, Oita Prefecture|Hayasuihime Shrine (Saiki)|}} {{神社 |名称 = Hayasuihime Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hayasuihime-jinja_Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|qid=Q133924|1=Ōita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oita|Q199609|qid=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}}{{ill|lt=Saganoseki-chō|Q7398999|qid=Q7398999|lt=Saganoseki|1=Saganoseki, Ōita}}3336-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Yasomagatsuhi-no-Kami|Q11591203|qid=Q11591203|lt=Yasomagatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ōnaobi-no-Kami|Q11581794|qid=Q11581794|lt=Ōnaobi-no-Kami|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokotsutsu-no-O, Nakatsutsu-no-O, Uwatsutsu-no-O)
Various deities of earth and sea |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#various shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=BCE|Q28200|qid=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}} 667 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|qid=Q2717|1=July 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Hayasuihime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Hayasuihime-jinja_Honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] [[File:Hayasuihime-jinja_Somon.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Main gate]] '''Hayasuihime Shrine''' (はやすいひめじんじゃ, はやすひめじんじゃ; also written '''Hayasuhime Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Oita|Q199609|qid=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|qid=Q133924|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, with former rank {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} of prefectural shrine. Also called "Seki no Gongen-sama." It enshrines {{ill|lt=Yasomagatsuhi-no-Kami|Q11591203|qid=Q11591203|lt=Yasomagatsuhi-no-Kami|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}, {{ill|lt=Ōnaobi-no-Kami|Q11581794|qid=Q11581794|lt=Ōnaobi-no-Kami|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}}, the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokotsutsu-no-kami, Nakatsutsu-no-kami, Uwatsutsu-no-kami), and various deities of earth and sea. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=BCE|Q28200|qid=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}} 667, when Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}) passed through Hayasui Strait ({{ill|lt=Hōyo Strait|Q867370|qid=Q867370|1=Hōyo Strait}}) during the {{ill|lt=Jimmu Eastern Expedition|Q11590444|qid=Q11590444|lt=eastern campaign|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, two divine ama sisters, Isago and Masago, retrieved from the seafloor a sacred sword guarded by a giant {{ill|lt=octopus|Q40152|qid=Q40152|1=Octopus}}, calmed the tides, and offered it to him. He then made that sword the sacred object and enshrined the deity of purification gates (Hayasuihime), praying for national foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|qid=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 (701), the shrine was moved to its current site. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5 (1600), the shrine buildings were burned in war, but after becoming territory of {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|qid=Q1194248|1=Kumamoto Domain}}, they were rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|Q1069843|qid=Q1069843|1=Katō Kiyomasa}} in Keichō 7 (1602), and later rebuilt by successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Hosokawa clan|Q2736120|qid=Q2736120|1=Hosokawa clan}}. In March 2004, the honden, main gate, and shrine household building were designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Oita Prefecture. The octopus that long guarded the sacred sword is treated as a shrine attendant spirit, and shrine priests never eat octopus. Even now, worshippers dedicate octopus drawings inscribed with wishes in a "tako-dachi" vow rite[https://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/2/view/302 Uwa Sea and folk culture (FY1992), (1) East and west of Hayasui Strait (Sadamisaki and Sekizaki)] Lifelong Learning Information System, "Ehime no Kioku". A wisteria festival is held every year in early May[https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/12393 Hayasuihime Shrine Wisteria Festival] Tourism Oita, and a grand summer festival is held in July, with floats and mikoshi parading through the town. The haiden roof preserves a local tile technique and features unique roof tiles such as Urashima Tarō and a three-story pagoda. === Cultural properties === * Oita Prefecture designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Hayasuihime Shrine Honden (designated March 31, 2004) ** Hayasuihime Shrine Main Gate (designated March 31, 2004) ** Hayasuihime Shrine shrine household (Ono family residence) (designated March 31, 2004) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsukiyama Kofun (Oita)|Q11603501|qid=Q11603501|1=Tsukiyama Kofun}} - one theory says this is the tomb of "Hayasuihime" * {{ill|lt=List of Oita Prefecture designated cultural properties|Q11432813|qid=Q11432813|1=List of Oita Prefecture designated cultural properties}} === External links === * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/4629 Hayasuihime Shrine] Official tourism site of Oita Prefecture * [http://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/CPID:55062 Hayasuhime Shrine] Oita City Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hayasuihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hayasuihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hayasuihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Oita Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]
